title: PM Org DDL Draft Packet History Demotion Execution
objective: Normalize the remaining live-looking 2026-04-14 PM org-schema DDL packet-definition singleton so that file no longer presents itself as a still-live PM org-schema DDL packet after standalone repo cutover and instead acts as historical packet provenance with an explicit current-routing redirect.
dependency_note: After Packet 227 closes the adjacent `pm-schema-011a` singleton, the nearest remaining current-looking residue in the same PM domain draft lane is the successor `pm-schema-011b` org-schema DDL singleton. Its surrounding packet-history now serves only as provenance, so the packet-definition JSON itself is the strongest adjacent live-looking residue.
execution_scope: Retitle the `pm-schema-011b` packet-definition singleton as historical provenance.
Add explicit `historical_note` and `current_routing` fields to the packet JSON.
Preserve the original packet body below those top-entry routing fields without reopening DDL authoring, validation, or runtime scope.
forbidden_scope: broad packet-archive rewriting beyond top-entry demotion and routing normalization
success_criteria: The `pm-schema-011b` packet-definition singleton now reads as historical provenance instead of a live PM org-schema DDL packet.
The file explicitly routes current readers to `PROJECT_STATUS.md` and the closeout inventory.
Repo-owned status routing records the closure and leaves no ambiguity about the next remaining adjacent residue check.
next_action: Reassess any smaller adjacent packet-history surfaces that still read as current after the `pm-schema-011b` singleton closure.
